untranslatable_reason: Pattern identification (Bump-and-Run Reversal Bottom visual formation) requires candlestick pattern recognition beyond available indicators. The specific pattern characteristics (initial bump followed by run, cup-with-handle-like or rounding bottom shape) cannot be expressed using only price-based indicators like moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger Bands, MACD, etc. A true implementation would require pattern matching algorithms or custom shape-detection logic not available in the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: Provided a simplified uptrend breakout rule as fallback using price crossing above SMA_20 for entry and crossing below for exit. This captures the general upward reversal concept but does NOT properly encode the specific BARR Bottom pattern morphology. Average rise of 31-38% suggests holding for trend reversal; stop loss set conservatively at 5% to account for pattern volatility. Exit conditions marked as AUTHOR_SILENT in original, so trend reversal used as reasonable alternative.
